title: How to Connect a Channel Manager
summary: Step by step guide to connecting your channel manager to GAS using the setup wizard.
content:
GAS connects to your channel manager so your properties, rooms, pricing and availability sync automatically. Supported channel managers include Beds24, Hostaway, Hostfully, Smoobu, Lodgify and more.

STEP 1 — Start the connection wizard
1. In GAS Admin, go to Channel Manager
2. Click Connections
3. Click Add Connection
4. Select your channel manager from the list
5. The setup wizard will open — follow each step

STEP 2 — Enter your credentials
Each channel manager requires different credentials. Here is where to find them:

Beds24:
- Log in to your Beds24 account
- Go to Account > API Keys
- Copy your API Key
- Paste into the GAS wizard

Hostaway:
- Log in to your Hostaway account
- Go to Settings > API
- Copy your Account ID and API Key
- Paste both into the GAS wizard

Hostfully:
- Log in to your Hostfully account
- Go to Settings > Integrations > API
- Copy your API Key
- Paste into the GAS wizard

Smoobu / Lodgify:
- Log in to your account
- Go to Settings > API or Integrations
- Copy your API Key
- Paste into the GAS wizard

STEP 3 — Map your properties
1. After entering credentials, GAS will fetch your properties from the channel manager
2. Select which properties to import into GAS
3. GAS will map rooms and rates automatically

STEP 4 — Complete the sync
1. Click Finish or Complete in the wizard
2. GAS will run an initial sync — this may take a few minutes
3. Go to Channel Manager > Connections to see your connection status
4. A green 'Connected' badge means everything is working

If the connection fails:
- Double check your API key was copied correctly with no extra spaces
- Make sure your channel manager account is active
- Contact GAS support at hello@gas.travel with your channel manager name

Need help? Contact the GAS team at hello@gas.travel

title: How to Add and Edit Rooms
summary: How to add, edit and manage rooms and units within your properties in GAS.
content:
Rooms and units are the bookable spaces within your property. Each room can have its own description, images, pricing and availability.

STEP 1 — Go to Rooms
1. In GAS Admin, go to Property Management
2. Click Rooms & Units
3. You will see all rooms for your properties

STEP 2 — Add a new room
1. Click Add Room or the + button
2. Select which property this room belongs to
3. Enter room details:
   - Room name (e.g. 'Double Room', 'Studio Apartment')
   - Room type (Entire Place, Private Room, Shared Room)
   - Maximum guests
   - Number of bedrooms
   - Number of bathrooms
   - Description (supports multiple languages)
4. Click Save

STEP 3 — Add room images
1. Go to Property Management > Images
2. Select your room
3. Upload photos — use high quality images for best results
4. Drag to reorder — the first image is the main photo shown in search results

STEP 4 — Add amenities
1. Go to Property Management > Amenities
2. Select your room or property
3. Tick the amenities that apply
4. Save

STEP 5 — Edit an existing room
1. In Rooms & Units, find your room
2. Click Edit
3. Make your changes
4. Click Save

Note: If you use a channel manager, room details sync automatically from there. You can still edit the GAS description and images separately — these are used for your GAS website and booking pages.

Need help? Contact the GAS team at hello@gas.travel

title: How to Create and Deploy Your Website
summary: How to create and deploy a GAS-powered booking website for your property.
content:
GAS creates a complete booking website for your property — with rooms, pricing, availability calendar, and online booking. Here is how to set it up.

STEP 1 — Create your site
1. In GAS Admin, go to Create Websites
2. Click Create Sites
3. Click Create New Site or Add Site
4. Enter your site details:
   - Site name (your property or business name)
   - Subdomain (e.g. 'myhotel' creates myhotel.sites.gas.travel)
   - Select which property this site is for
5. Click Create

STEP 2 — Your site is live
Your site is immediately available at yourname.sites.gas.travel. Share this address with guests while you set up your custom domain.

STEP 3 — Customise your site
1. Find your site in Create Websites
2. Click Settings or Edit
3. You can customise:
   - Site name and tagline
   - Colours and branding
   - Hero image and headline
   - About section
   - Contact details
   - Navigation menu
   - Language settings
4. Save after each change — changes appear on your site within a minute

STEP 4 — Connect your custom domain (optional)
To use your own domain (e.g. myhotel.com) instead of the .sites.gas.travel address, see the article: How to Connect Your Custom Domain.

STEP 5 — Check your booking is working
1. Visit your site
2. Select a room and click Book Now
3. Choose dates and complete a test booking
4. Confirm the booking appears in Bookings & Revenue > Bookings

If the booking button does not work, check:
- Your channel manager connection is active (green status)
- Your payment method is configured in Bookings & Revenue > Payments

Need help? Contact the GAS team at hello@gas.travel

title: How to Manage Bookings
summary: How to view, manage and track bookings in GAS.
content:
All bookings from your GAS website and connected channel managers appear in the Bookings section.

STEP 1 — View your bookings
1. In GAS Admin, go to Bookings & Revenue
2. Click Bookings
3. You will see a list of all bookings with:
   - Guest name
   - Property and room
   - Check-in and check-out dates
   - Booking status
   - Payment status
   - Total amount

STEP 2 — View booking details
1. Click on any booking to see full details:
   - Guest contact information
   - Special requests
   - Upsells selected
   - Payment breakdown
   - Taxes and fees

STEP 3 — Booking statuses
- Confirmed — booking is active
- Pending — awaiting confirmation or payment
- Cancelled — booking has been cancelled
- Completed — guest has checked out

STEP 4 — Filter and search bookings
Use the filters at the top to:
- Search by guest name
- Filter by date range
- Filter by property
- Filter by status

STEP 5 — Channel manager bookings
If you use a channel manager (Beds24, Hostaway etc), bookings made through Airbnb, Booking.com or your other channels also appear here after syncing.

Important: To modify or cancel a booking that came through a channel manager, make the change in your channel manager — it will sync back to GAS automatically.

Need help? Contact the GAS team at hello@gas.travel

title: How to Create Voucher Codes
summary: How to create discount voucher codes that guests can use at checkout.
content:
Vouchers are discount codes guests enter at checkout to get a reduction on their booking price.

STEP 1 — Create a voucher
1. In GAS Admin, go to Bookings & Revenue
2. Click Vouchers
3. Click Add Voucher
4. Enter voucher details:
   - Voucher Code — the code guests type in (e.g. SUMMER20)
   - Display Name — shown to guests at checkout (e.g. 'Summer Sale 20%')
   - Description — optional extra info shown to guests
   - Discount type — percentage or fixed amount
   - Discount value — e.g. 20 for 20% off
   - Valid from / Valid to — date range the code works
   - Usage limit — how many times it can be used (optional)
5. Click Save

STEP 2 — Add translations (optional)
If your site is multilingual, click the language flag tabs to add translations for the display name and description in each language.

STEP 3 — Share with guests
Share the voucher code directly with guests by email, social media or any other channel. Guests enter it in the Promo Code field at checkout.

STEP 4 — Track usage
In the Vouchers list you can see how many times each code has been used.

Need help? Contact the GAS team at hello@gas.travel
